skills.name: js
skills.text: JavaScript is one of my most used languages. I have used it in many different web development courses and projects. It might be my favourite coding language.

skills.name: react
skills.text: My React journey started with an overwhelming and confusing attempt to make sense of it. But I was left with a curiosity and iterest to learn more, so that's extacly what I did.

skills.name: html
skills.text: HTML and CSS where the languages that sparked my love for web development. Seeing concrete results of my coding in a browser excited me a lot. Learning React later on only added on to that excitement.

skills.name: node
skills.text: I first learned to use Node.js in a backend development course where we built a backend server and a database for a user to keep track of expenses. I was very excited about the fact that now I could build a whole full stack web page.

skills.name: java
skills.text: Java was the first ever coding language that I learned. Even though it's not the language I use the most now, it will always have a special place in my heart.

skills.name: python
skills.text: I learned Pyhton by doing a online class as an extra learning experience, but it quickly became one of my favourite languages

skills.name: c-sharp
skills.text: I learned C# in my first year of studying, straight after learing Java. We used it to build and develop a mobile game with Unity. It was challenging to use a whole new language with very little experience over all and perform well in a big and important client project. But eventually I got the hang of it which shows in the end results ;).

skills.name: git
skills.text: I have been using Git with GitHub troughout my whole studies and I feel like I have become relatively fluent with it.
